title: Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides
intro: Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides, also known as the Chinese crown daisy or crown daisy, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as tinctures and capsules, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other infectious diseases.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that are often characterized by their composite inflorescences. Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides is further classified within the genus Chrysanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia and Europe. The specific classification of Chrysanthemum argyranthemoides also places it within the subgenus Lepidospermum. Further research may be necessary to determine the most up-to-date classification.
